high_yield_topics.topic: Climate Change, Biodiversity and Green Tech
Fiscal Policy, Revenue, and Resource Allocation
Public Health, Biotechnology and DNA Research

patterns: Judicial expansion of Fundamental Rights into everyday urban governance (Right to Walk)
Conflict between National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 linguistic mandates and regional/tribal federalism
Integration of AI-driven biometric tools (NCRB Abhigyan) in policing vs. civil liberty concerns
Shift from primary renewable generation to grid optimization and transmission bottlenecks (HTLS conductors)
Rising focus on secondary pollutants (Surface Ozone) linked to climate-driven heatwaves
Financialization of tech through mega-IPOs and SEBI regulatory shifts in buybacks
academy_insights.filter_prompt:
Classify news as RELEVANT or NOT for the UPSC Civil Services Exam based on the following specific high-yield criteria:

1. GS-I (Society & Geography): Focus on geophysical changes such as glacial lake expansion (GLOF risks) and social empowerment like women-dedicated policing and PVTG-specific health crises (Sickle Cell Anaemia). 

2. GS-II (Governance & IR): Prioritize judicial activism extending Article 19 and 21 (e.g., Right to Walk), federal friction regarding the Three-Language Formula/NEP, and India's leadership in multilateral bodies (FATF VP, UNHRC). Any news regarding the IT Act Section 69A or surveillance technology must be analyzed for its impact on civil liberties.

3. GS-III (Economy, Environment, & Tech): Focus on grid optimization techniques (HTLS

active_focus_areas: Federalism vs. NEP 2020 (Language Policy & State Rights)
NCRB Biometric Identification vs. Privacy (Abhigyan App)
Capital Market Reforms (Mega-IPOs & SEBI Buyback Norms)
Sickle Cell Elimination & Tribal Health Policy
